bullets.text: Q2 CR 93.4 — very acceptable despite $125M in aggregate storm losses from record severe weather quarter; catastrophe losses manageable within current pricing structure; result substantially better than industry-wide CR.

Written premium grew 3% in Q2, policies grew 5%; gap reflects ongoing headwind from flat-to-lower average written premium per policy driven by protracted frequency decline and aging vehicle population.

Catastrophe losses $125M for quarter — elevated but contained; frequency and severity changes (ex-comprehensive storm losses) unremarkable vs. Q1; several large personal auto states returning to profitable growth after prior adjustments.

First policy sold via mobile device in July; mobile payments and search activity growing rapidly; Snapshot available in 38 markets — Direct consumer acceptance ahead of agency; marketing support for Snapshot fully rolled out mid-Q2.

Snapshot rollout continued through Q2 with consumer acceptance in line with expectations; home+auto bundling promoted via new TV creative; mobile feature development roadmap presented at June 17 Investor Relations meeting.

Commercial Auto first positive written premium growth quarter (+3%) in 'some considerable time' — prior rate actions earning through; several large personal auto states in notably healthier growth and profitability position.

Significant rate activity by state continuing including increases; large states adjusted to reflect PIP trends from prior year now positioned for profitable growth; ongoing macro pressure from low premium inflation environment.

Catastrophe deployment logistics and staff commitment highlighted — hundreds of people leaving regular routines to serve customers in disaster areas; catastrophe response described as a point of organizational pride.

'Messenger' campaign debuted in many markets in Q2 — strong early acceptance numbers; complements Flo by providing additional perspective on products; additional commercial inventory and digital derivatives added.
Expense management emphasis amplified by flat-to-no premium inflation environment; retention focus intensifying as renewals become relatively more important to growth vs. new business acquisition.
